prompt: Photo-realistic editorial café still-life shot from above. Keep the existing scene exactly as-is: the blue marble bistro table with gilded scalloped edge, the cappuccino with latte art and biscuit on a white saucer with spoon, the bentwood cane café chair, the grey cobblestone street, the warm natural daylight and shallow depth of field. Preserve the original coffee, table, chair and lighting pixels. Only two things change: replace the canvas tote bag, and replace the flowers. The bag is a medium chocolate-brown pebbled-leather duffle bag with a zip top and short handles, sitting upright on the chair seat. The flowers are a simple bunch of soft pink and cream ranunculus with green foliage, the kind from a corner grocer, wrapped loosely. Render with authentic phone-camera realism: real fabric grain, true-to-life shadows and soft window light, slight lens vignetting, no over-smoothing, no plastic sheen, no warped geometry, correct number of straps and handles, legible undistorted leather stitching, no extra limbs or duplicated objects, no text artifacts. Keep colors true — do not push the frame warm or pink.
